title: SQL injection via string concatenation
description: User input from req.params.id is directly concatenated into a SQL query string without parameterization in the GET /api/users/:id endpoint.
remediation: Use parameterized queries: pool.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1', [req.params.id])

title: SQL injection via string concatenation in admin delete endpoint
description: User input from req.params.id is directly concatenated into a DELETE SQL query without parameterization.
remediation: Use parameterized queries: pool.query('DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $1', [req.params.id])

title: Reflected XSS via unsanitized query parameter
description: User input from req.query.q is directly interpolated into an HTML response string without escaping or sanitization.
remediation: Sanitize user input before embedding in HTML using DOMPurify or escape HTML entities.

title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) via user-controlled URL
description: The POST /api/fetch-url endpoint passes req.body.url directly to fetch() without any URL validation or allowlist.
remediation: Validate and restrict the URL against an allowlist of permitted domains. Block private IP ranges.

title: Missing authorization on admin delete endpoint
description: The DELETE /api/admin/users/:id endpoint has no authentication or authorization middleware.
remediation: Add authentication middleware and authorization checks (role === 'admin') before processing.

title: Hardcoded JWT secret in source code
description: The JWT signing secret is hardcoded. An attacker with source access can forge valid JWT tokens.
remediation: Move the JWT secret to an environment variable with cryptographically random value.

title: Hardcoded API key in source code
description: An API key is hardcoded, exposed to anyone with source code access.
remediation: Move the API key to an environment variable. Use a secrets manager in production.

title: Hardcoded database credentials in source code
description: Database connection string with credentials is hardcoded in source.
remediation: Move the database connection string to process.env.DATABASE_URL.

title: Secrets exposed in .env file committed to repository
description: The .env file contains plaintext secrets: DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, and API_KEY.
remediation: Add .env to .gitignore. Rotate all exposed credentials.

title: JWT token issued without expiration
description: The jwt.sign() call does not set expiresIn. Tokens never expire.

title: Permissive CORS configuration allows all origins
description: The app uses cors() with no configuration, setting Access-Control-Allow-Origin to '*'.
remediation: Configure CORS with specific origin allowlist.

title: Sensitive data (password) written to logs
description: The /api/register endpoint logs the user's plaintext password via console.log.
remediation: Never log sensitive data. Remove password from log statement.

title: No rate limiting on login endpoint
description: The POST /api/login endpoint has no rate limiting, enabling brute-force attacks.
remediation: Add rate limiting: rateLimit({ windowMs: 15*60*1000, max: 5 })
